summary: Matched the common fighter Ottotto/OttottoWait code by moving a block of ft_08A4 assembly into a new C translation unit, wiring it into the build, adding the public header, and exposing supporting symbols/flags needed for the match. The report shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Ottotto.c at 100.00% with all listed functions matched.
what_changed: Added new source file src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Ottotto.c implementing the previously-assembled Ottotto and OttottoWait routines.
Removed the corresponding matched assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, leaving later assembly such as ft_8009A804 in place.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Ottotto.h with declarations for the new ftCommon Ottotto callbacks/functions.
Added ftCo_Ottotto.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C file is compiled into the text object list.
Promoted the local assembly label .L_800C94B4 to a global symbol named ftCo_800C94B4 so C code can call it from ftCo_Ottotto_IASA.
Added MPCOLL_FLAGS_B23 as a named collision flag in src/common_structs.h and used it in ft_8009A3C8.
Updated fighter-related headers/types/action wiring so the new Ottotto functions and motion-state callbacks integrate with the existing ftCommon action table.
Updated tools/functions.sh, likely to include the new matched file/function group in project tooling; the exact tooling behavior is not visible in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Split the matched block into a dedicated ftCommon file named after the action, ftCo_Ottotto.c, instead of leaving it embedded in the broad ft_08A4 assembly unit.
Kept helper transitions static where possible, with file-local helpers ftCo_8009A410 and ftCo_8009A6B8 matching the original internal branch targets.
Used the existing RETURN_IF pattern to express the long IASA input-priority chain cleanly while preserving the assembly behavior of early exit after the first accepted action.
Named an otherwise raw collision bit as MPCOLL_FLAGS_B23 before using it in C, improving readability and reducing magic-number dependence.
Exposed the still-unmatched .L_800C94B4 assembly target as ftCo_800C94B4 instead of duplicating or guessing its implementation, allowing the current match to progress without overreaching.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack artifacts with MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays and explicit @todo comments rather than hiding them in normal logic.

Used existing motion/collision helpers such as Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ft_800827A0, ft_800CC730, mpLib_80053FF4, mpLib_80054158, and ft_8008A2BC to recover intent while matching codegen.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action-state files, a clean match often requires both code migration and action-table/header/build updates; moving assembly alone is not enough.
When a matched function calls a still-assembly local label, promote that label to a named global symbol and declare it rather than forcing a speculative decomp of unrelated code.
Long IASA handlers can be represented as ordered RETURN_IF chains; the order is semantically important because each check may consume input and return early.
Collision decomp benefits from naming flag bits as soon as their use is known, even when the high-level semantic name is not yet fully understood.
MUST_MATCH guards are appropriate for preserving compiler stack layout quirks such as unused stack space, while keeping non-matching builds readable.
Motion-state transition helpers often need to zero velocities or reset animation/collision state immediately after Fighter_ChangeMotionState to reproduce action setup.
Small no-op callbacks, such as Phys or Wait_Anim functions that compile to blr, should still be represented in C when they are part of the action callback table.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h, here ftCo_Ottotto.c and ftCo_Ottotto.h.
Action callbacks follow the pattern ftCo_Ottotto_Anim, ftCo_Ottotto_IASA, ftCo_Ottotto_Phys, ftCo_Ottotto_Coll and similarly ftCo_OttottoWait_*.
Unknown but address-stable ftCommon functions use address-derived names such as ftCo_8009A410, ftCo_8009A6B8, and ftCo_800C94B4.
Legacy or less-classified fighter functions may retain ft_<address> names, such as ft_8009A3C8.
Collision flag constants use the MPCOLL_FLAGS_BNN style when the exact meaning is not yet known, as with MPCOLL_FLAGS_B23.
Motion-state enum names use ftCo_MS_<Action>, as shown by ftCo_MS_Ottotto.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced roughly 322 deleted assembly lines from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s with 165 lines of C in ftCo_Ottotto.c while preserving a 100.00% match report.
Used MUST_MATCH-only dummy stack arrays in ftCo_Ottotto_IASA and collision functions to reproduce unused stack allocation in the original code.
Matched empty callback functions as empty C bodies, producing direct returns for ftCo_Ottotto_Phys, ftCo_OttottoWait_Anim, and ftCo_OttottoWait_Phys.
Used explicit float/velocity assignments after Fighter_ChangeMotionState to match stores to self_vel.x/y/z and gr_vel.
Recovered collision edge checks by comparing fighter position against floor edge positions fetched through mpLib_80053FF4 or mpLib_80054158 depending on facing direction.
Kept unknown helper names address-based instead of inventing semantic names where evidence was insufficient.
Moved sdata2 constants out of the assembly excerpt as part of replacing the matched block, relying on C-generated constants for NULL/1.0f behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.

The PR body/report explicitly showed every listed function in ftCo_Ottotto.c at 100.00%, including ftCo_Ottotto_IASA, ftCo_Ottotto_Coll, ftCo_OttottoWait_Coll, ftCo_8009A6B8, ftCo_8009A410, ft_8009A3C8, ftCo_Ottotto_Anim, ftCo_OttottoWait_IASA, ftCo_OttottoWait_Anim, ftCo_OttottoWait_Phys, and ftCo_Ottotto_Phys.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_8009A410, ftCo_8009A6B8, or ftCo_800C94B4 to semantic names.
Search for uses or semantic clarification of MPCOLL_FLAGS_B23 in later collision or fighter PRs.
Inspect later ftCommon action PRs for reuse of RETURN_IF IASA chains and MUST_MATCH unused stack padding.
Check whether tools/functions.sh changes in this PR affected function report generation or object slicing.
Look for subsequent cleanup of ftCo_OttottoWait_Coll and ftCo_Ottotto_Coll duplication.
